added_assumptions.label: A-quant-margin
added_assumptions.statement: (Quantitative margin, §7.) For every observable law P in the open class O and every admissible direction w, the cumulant-envelope support loss chi_w(P) := h_{R_LP(P)}(w) - sup_{Sigma in R_LP(P) cap C_eps(P)} <w, Sigma> is strictly positive, i.e. chi_w(P) > 0.
added_assumptions.source: Already present at lines 356-371 of eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_v1.tex; relabelled here so the orchestrator records it in state.added_assumptions and the demoted unconditional Conjecture in §8 documents what generic-class strict-gap result is left open.

flags.rewound_from_stage0: Case 2c (Bucket A — regime_defining) + regression pattern (a) same flag — the proposal silently promoted the strict-gap kernel into Assumption ass:quant-margin and then 'confirmed' Conjecture 1 via the envelope lemma; reviewer correctly flags the derived content as subfield. Theorem-split is the autonomous-safe path: demote the unconditional strict-gap to an open Conjecture in §8 and state the conditional-on-ass:quant-margin theorem as the §7 main result. theorem_splits=0, so loop guard not triggered.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp non-equivalence theorem for local-projection (LP) versus structural-vector-autoregression (SVAR) identification of structural impulse responses under non-Gaussian innovations and partial sign restrictions. Plagborg-Moller and Wolf (2021, Econometrica) established that LP and SVAR estimate the same population impulse response under finite-order linear VAR data-generating processes when both use the same identifying restrictions; Montiel Olea, Plagborg-Moller, Qian, and Wolf (Econometrica, forthcoming) extend the equivalence to local projections under misspecification of lag length. The flagship question: characterize the sharp boundary at which LP and SVAR identify distinct structural impulse responses when innovations are non-Gaussian with bounded higher-cumulant restrictions a la Gourieroux-Monfort-Renne (2017) and Lanne-Meitz-Saikkonen (2017), and when the structural impact matrix is restricted only by a partial sign/zero pattern. The kernel claim is a closed-form algebraic non-equivalence theorem: under non-Gaussian innovations with bounded fourth cumulant gap, the LP-IRF and SVAR-IRF identify distinct functionals of the structural-shock distribution unless a cumulant-matching restriction is jointly imposed, recovering Plagborg-Moller-Wolf equivalence at the Gaussian limit.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains adjacent rather than duplicative: q1_spectral_phase_transition studies panel row-space/singular-subspace boundaries, while pid_network_exposure and pid_dose_response_iv study shared-completion/common-latent pasting failures. None states an LP/SVAR, structural-IRF, non-Gaussian fourth-cumulant, or sign-restricted support-function boundary. The v4 gap is sharper: Conjecture 1 asks for a primitive open-class proof of strict LP/sign versus SVAR/sign separation rather than an assumed support margin.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are PlagborgMollerWolf2021 for LP/VAR portability under common identifying restrictions, LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017 and GourierouxMonfortRenne2017 for non-Gaussian SVAR identification from a single innovation law, Guay2021 for higher-moment rank identification, and DrautzburgWright2023 for independence-refined sign-restricted VAR sets. The flagship ε-gap is a primitive generic support-function separation for an LP/sign relaxation versus one common non-Gaussian SVAR law; Theorem 2 is now explicitly conditional and not counted as proving that primitive margin."}
seed_list: Prior work PlagborgMollerWolf2021 has established that LP and VAR identification schemes are portable when both target the same population IRF object. Our seed conjectures that portability fails for a horizonwise LP relaxation under non-Gaussian sign restrictions because one fourth-cumulant tensor must be diagonalized by a single shock rotation across all horizons.

Prior work GourierouxMonfortRenne2017 has established that independent non-Gaussian shocks remove the usual SVAR rotation non-identifiability. Our seed conjectures that LP horizonwise cumulant bounds are only an outer relaxation because marginal fourth-cumulant admissibility need not paste into one independent-component law.

Prior work LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017 has established essentially unique non-Gaussian SVAR impulse responses under independent structural shocks. Our seed conjectures that the uniqueness can create strict LP/SVAR non-equivalence because LP projection rows can satisfy the same signs while selecting a different non-diagonalizing rotation at another horizon.

Prior work DrautzburgWright2023 has established that independence can refine sign-restricted VAR sets but may yield local, non-convex, weakly identified sets. Our seed conjectures that the LP/SVAR frontier is a strict non-rectangularity phenomenon because optimizing sign restrictions and independence horizon by horizon overstates the joint feasible response set.

Prior work MontielOleaPlagborgMollerQianWolf2024 has established that LP confidence intervals can remain valid under local VAR lag misspecification while short VAR intervals undercover. Our seed conjectures that non-Gaussian SVAR identification has an additional population-level fragility because lag truncation distorts the fourth-cumulant diagonalization that labels shocks.

Prior work MontielOleaStockWatson2021 has established weak-instrument robust confidence sets for proxy-SVAR impulse responses. Our seed conjectures that adding weak non-Gaussian cumulant gaps to weak proxies creates a three-way weak-identification boundary that cannot be represented by proxy-LP Fieller sets alone.

Prior work AriasRubioRamirezWaggoner2021 has assumed proxy, sign, and zero restrictions can jointly label structural shocks in Bayesian proxy-SVAR workflows. Our seed conjectures that adding weak higher-cumulant independence creates a non-convex frequentist projection problem whose LP relaxation can include response paths with no common proxy-cumulant completion.

Prior work LanneLiu2026 argues that mutual independence is stronger than standard SVAR orthogonality and that skewness or excess kurtosis can identify some shocks without independence. Our seed conjectures that orthogonal-but-dependent shocks preserve some sign-restricted LP equivalences while breaking independence-refined SVAR equivalence only along rank-deficient cumulant directions.

Prior work Guay2021 has established local GMM identification from higher-moment rank conditions. Our seed conjectures that the LP/SVAR equality boundary is exactly the failure of those rank conditions after projecting the same cumulant moments through horizon-specific LP rows.

Prior work LiPlagborgMollerWolf2024 has documented estimator bias-variance differences between LP and VAR conclusions across horizons. Our seed conjectures a sharper population phenomenon: even before sampling error, non-Gaussian sign restrictions can make the LP and SVAR identified response sets differ because horizonwise LP rotations do not share one structural innovation law.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work PlagborgMollerWolf2021 has established that LP and VAR identification schemes are portable when both target the same population IRF object. Our seed conjectures that portability fails for a horizonwise LP relaxation under non-Gaussian sign restrictions because one fourth-cumulant tensor must be diagonalized by a single shock rotation across all horizons.

anchor_paper.author: Mikkel Plagborg-Moller and Christian K. Wolf
anchor_paper.claim: LPs and VARs estimate the same impulse responses under unrestricted lag structures and portable sign restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/non-equivalence against named PlagborgMollerWolf2021 portability claim; not blocked because the obstruction is a common fourth-cumulant law on an open nondegenerate region, not a hand-tuned zero outcome.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from PlagborgMollerWolf2021 because they already say sign restrictions can equivalently be implemented with LPs.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their equivalence assumes a common structural estimand; it does not compare a horizonwise LP relaxation with one cumulant-diagonalizing shock law.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work GourierouxMonfortRenne2017 has established that independent non-Gaussian shocks remove the usual SVAR rotation non-identifiability. Our seed conjectures that LP horizonwise cumulant bounds are only an outer relaxation because marginal fourth-cumulant admissibility need not paste into one independent-component law.

anchor_paper.author: Christian Gourieroux, Alain Monfort, and Jean-Paul Renne
anchor_paper.claim: Independent-component methods remove SVAR rotation non-identifiability under independent non-Gaussian shocks.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension from ICA-SVAR to LP comparison; not blocked because the extension creates a new pasting failure absent from the SVAR-only paper.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ICA identification because any admissible LP cumulant vector can be represented by some SVAR rotation.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: ICA supplies a common rotation when the full innovation law is used, but it does not show horizonwise LP admissible marginals paste into one rotation.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017 has established essentially unique non-Gaussian SVAR impulse responses under independent structural shocks. Our seed conjectures that the uniqueness can create strict LP/SVAR non-equivalence because LP projection rows can satisfy the same signs while selecting a different non-diagonalizing rotation at another horizon.

anchor_paper.author: Markku Lanne, Mika Meitz, and Pentti Saikkonen
anchor_paper.claim: Independent non-Gaussian SVAR shocks identify the impact matrix up to scale and permutation.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/non-equivalence against named LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017 uniqueness implication; not blocked because the witness separates LP projection rows from the unique common SVAR rotation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017 because uniqueness of the impact matrix makes every method target the same response.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: uniqueness can sharpen the SVAR set relative to LP rows that satisfy signs without diagonalizing the same cumulant tensor.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DrautzburgWright2023 has established that independence can refine sign-restricted VAR sets but may yield local, non-convex, weakly identified sets. Our seed conjectures that the LP/SVAR frontier is a strict non-rectangularity phenomenon because optimizing sign restrictions and independence horizon by horizon overstates the joint feasible response set.

anchor_paper.author: Thorsten Drautzburg and Jonathan H. Wright
anchor_paper.claim: Independence can refine sign-restricted VAR sets, but sets may be local, non-convex, and weakly identified.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Non-rectangularity motif; not blocked because the target method is named (DrautzburgWright2023 independence-refined sign restrictions) and the obstruction is an open joint feasible-set gap.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DrautzburgWright2023 because their independence-refined set is already the sharp sign-restricted set.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: they refine VAR rotations with independence but do not define or compare the horizonwise LP relaxation or common-cumulant pasting boundary.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MontielOleaPlagborgMollerQianWolf2024 has established that LP confidence intervals can remain valid under local VAR lag misspecification while short VAR intervals undercover. Our seed conjectures that non-Gaussian SVAR identification has an additional population-level fragility because lag truncation distorts the fourth-cumulant diagonalization that labels shocks.

anchor_paper.author: Jose Luis Montiel Olea, Mikkel Plagborg-Moller, Eric Qian, and Christian K. Wolf
anchor_paper.claim: LP confidence intervals retain coverage under local VAR misspecification while short finite-lag VAR intervals can undercover.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends misspecification robustness to non-Gaussian SVAR inference; thin unless it proves population-level cumulant distortion, so scored below top candidates.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from MontielOleaPlagborgMollerQianWolf2024 because LP robustness already handles VAR misspecification.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their result is about coverage under reduced-form lag misspecification, not population non-equivalence from higher-cumulant structural labels.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MontielOleaStockWatson2021 has established weak-instrument robust confidence sets for proxy-SVAR impulse responses. Our seed conjectures that adding weak non-Gaussian cumulant gaps to weak proxies creates a three-way weak-identification boundary that cannot be represented by proxy-LP Fieller sets alone.

anchor_paper.author: Jose Luis Montiel Olea, James H. Stock, and Mark W. Watson
anchor_paper.claim: External-instrument SVAR inference is analogous to weak-IV inference and supports weak-instrument robust confidence sets.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends weak-IV inference to weak cumulants; not blocked only if the result proves a new three-way weak-identification boundary, otherwise would be an inference extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from weak-IV SVAR inference because weak cumulants are just weak instruments in another notation.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: external-instrument relevance and non-Gaussian cumulant gaps are different moment systems whose joint weak boundary is not characterized there.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AriasRubioRamirezWaggoner2021 has assumed proxy, sign, and zero restrictions can jointly label structural shocks in Bayesian proxy-SVAR workflows. Our seed conjectures that adding weak higher-cumulant independence creates a non-convex frequentist projection problem whose LP relaxation can include response paths with no common proxy-cumulant completion.

anchor_paper.author: Jonas E. Arias, Juan F. Rubio-Ramirez, and Daniel F. Waggoner
anchor_paper.claim: Bayesian proxy-SVAR algorithms can combine proxies with zero and sign restrictions to jointly identify shocks.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends proxy/sign workflows with cumulants; not blocked only if it proves non-convex common-completion failure, otherwise too close to method combination.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from proxy-SVAR sign/zero algorithms because adding moment inequalities is routine.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: existing algorithms combine proxy/sign/zero restrictions but do not give a frequentist common cumulant-completion geometry.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work LanneLiu2026 argues that mutual independence is stronger than standard SVAR orthogonality and that skewness or excess kurtosis can identify some shocks without independence. Our seed conjectures that orthogonal-but-dependent shocks preserve some sign-restricted LP equivalences while breaking independence-refined SVAR equivalence only along rank-deficient cumulant directions.

anchor_paper.author: Markku Lanne and Keyan Liu
anchor_paper.claim: Orthogonality plus higher moments can identify some non-Gaussian shocks without mutual independence.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift seed; not blocked because orthogonal-but-dependent shocks can preserve or break LP/SVAR equivalence along rank-deficient cumulant directions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from LanneLiu2026 because they already characterize non-Gaussian identification without independence.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their orthogonal-dependent frontier does not include LP/SVAR equivalence or horizonwise projection restrictions.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Guay2021 has established local GMM identification from higher-moment rank conditions. Our seed conjectures that the LP/SVAR equality boundary is exactly the failure of those rank conditions after projecting the same cumulant moments through horizon-specific LP rows.

anchor_paper.author: Alain Guay
anchor_paper.claim: GMM approaches to non-Gaussian SVARs deliver local identification from higher-moment rank conditions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Rank-condition seed; not blocked because the proposed boundary is a failure of published higher-moment rank conditions after LP projection, not merely GMM applied to LPs.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Guay2021 because rank conditions already decide local identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Guay gives local identification conditions inside SVAR GMM, not equality between LP relaxation and SVAR common-law sets.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work LiPlagborgMollerWolf2024 has documented estimator bias-variance differences between LP and VAR conclusions across horizons. Our seed conjectures a sharper population phenomenon: even before sampling error, non-Gaussian sign restrictions can make the LP and SVAR identified response sets differ because horizonwise LP rotations do not share one structural innovation law.

anchor_paper.author: Dake Li, Mikkel Plagborg-Moller, and Christian K. Wolf
anchor_paper.claim: LP and VAR estimators occupy different bias-variance positions across DGPs, horizons, and identification designs.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Population-vs-estimator seed; not blocked because it predicts identified-set separation before sampling error, not another bias-variance comparison.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from LiPlagborgMollerWolf2024 because LP/VAR differences are already catalogued across horizons.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: that paper studies estimator performance for common targets, not a structural identified-set separation induced by non-Gaussian restrictions.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Jorda (2005, AER) introduced LP impulse-response estimation as the projection-side baseline.
Plagborg-Moller and Wolf (2021, Econometrica, PlagborgMollerWolf2021) prove LPs and VARs estimate the same impulse responses under unrestricted lag structures and portable identification schemes including sign restrictions.
Montiel Olea and Plagborg-Moller (2021, Econometrica, MontielOleaPlagborgMoller2021) establish robust LP inference for persistence and long horizons.
Montiel Olea, Plagborg-Moller, Qian, and Wolf (2024, Econometrica forthcoming, MontielOleaPlagborgMollerQianWolf2024) create the lag-misspecification robustness tension between LPs and finite-lag VARs.
Li, Plagborg-Moller, and Wolf (2024, Journal of Econometrics, LiPlagborgMollerWolf2024) show LP and VAR estimators occupy different bias-variance positions across DGPs and horizons.
Gourieroux, Monfort, and Renne (2017, Journal of Econometrics, GourierouxMonfortRenne2017) and Lanne, Meitz, and Saikkonen (2017, Journal of Econometrics, LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017) use non-Gaussian independence to identify SVAR shocks up to scale/permutation.
Guay (2021, JBES, Guay2021) gives GMM local identification via higher-moment rank conditions.
Drautzburg and Wright (2023, Journal of Econometrics, DrautzburgWright2023) refine sign-restricted VAR sets through independence and emphasize non-convexity plus weak higher-moment information.
Baumeister and Hamilton (2015, Econometrica, BaumeisterHamilton2015) and Arias, Rubio-Ramirez, and Waggoner (2018/2021 sign/proxy-SVAR thread) anchor the sign/zero restriction geometry.
Lanne and Liu (2026, Econometrics Journal, LanneLiu2026) relax mutual independence toward orthogonal but dependent non-Gaussian shocks, marking an active frontier.
The unresolved knot is whether LP/SVAR equivalence survives when LP restrictions are horizonwise but non-Gaussian SVAR restrictions require one common cumulant-diagonalizing innovation law.

banked_reason: Re-review under widened flagship rubric (axes b/c added) — Stage 0.5 still REJECT (route=user, theorem_splits cap hit + split_collapsed). Derivation reduces to one-line bookkeeping transfer R_SVAR(P) ⊆ R_LP(P) ∩ C_eps(P) after granting A-quant-margin; no axis-b non-equivalence threshold derived in closed form, no axis-c strict extension of named published regime. Math sound at field tier; flagship floor still unattainable in this kernel.
